label: AS 1851 — FIP monthly inspection
items: All mains-on, fault and alarm LEDs reading correctly
No outstanding faults on the panel display
Event log scrolled — no unresolved events since last visit
Battery voltage indication within range
Brigade signalling LED in the expected state
Spare fuses present and labelled
FIP cabinet locked, key in keysafe / register

label: AS 1851 — six-monthly functional test
items: Sample of 10% of detectors functionally tested (smoke / heat as appropriate)
All MCPs tested by sample, reset confirmed
Sounder operation confirmed, sound levels acceptable
Strobes operating where fitted
Brigade signalling test to monitoring centre — silent alarm
Mimic / sub-indicator panels reflecting FIP state
Cable entries, gland integrity visually inspected
Panel earthing continuity acceptable

label: AS 1851 — annual battery and full system
items: Standby battery discharge test under simulated alarm load
Battery voltage at end of discharge within manufacturer limits
Charger float voltage measured and recorded
100% of detectors functionally tested across a 12-month rolling sample
Cause-and-effect verification — selected scenarios
AS 1670.1 cable segregation visually re-checked
Logbook updated and signed
Defect register updated with any open items

label: Panel handover / commissioning
items: As-built drawings present at panel
Zone schedule labelled and matches AS 1670.1 zoning
Loop schedule present, addresses match installed devices
Battery sized per AS 1670.1 standby + alarm calculation
Brigade interface tested and signed off by monitoring centre
Cause-and-effect documented and witnessed
Handover pack delivered to client (drawings, manuals, certificates)
